title: One Sentence. A Codex That Feels Yours.

narration.direction: Warm, personable, conversational builder voice with gentle energy; human-first and clear, never commercial.

scenes.overlay: What if one sentence made Codex yours?
scenes.support: Describe it. Preview it. Apply it.
scenes.proof: One idea → a complete Codex theme
scenes.voiceover: What if one sentence could turn Codex into a workspace that feels unmistakably yours?

scenes.overlay: Say it. Name it. See both variants.
scenes.support: Voice or text becomes an original dark and light pair.
scenes.proof: Creative reference → semantic color roles → validated pair
scenes.voiceover: By voice or text, I ask Codex for dark and light themes inspired by Argentina football at night, and name it Albiazul Afterglow. GPT-5.6 interprets the brief; DexThemes validates both variants and makes sure every theme has a name.

scenes.overlay: Preview the whole workspace—not just swatches.
scenes.support: Full dark and light Codex mockups keep every color in context.
scenes.voiceover: Before I switch, DexThemes renders both variants as full Codex workspaces in the thread, not just swatches, so I can judge code, diffs, contrast, and accents in context.

scenes.overlay: No account between you and a new theme.
scenes.support: Create, preview, discover, and apply before signing in.
scenes.proof: Copy exact import · Open Appearance · You apply
scenes.voiceover: I copy the exact import, open Codex Appearance, and apply it. No account is required to create, preview, discover, or use a theme. Value comes before sign-in.

scenes.overlay: Find what fits—or what is winning.
scenes.support: Search every source, compare full previews, and follow community momentum.
scenes.proof: Today · this week · this month · all time
scenes.voiceover: I can search official, DexThemes, and community themes, compare full previews, and see what people love today, this week, this month, or all time.

scenes.overlay: Choose to publish. Get the credit.
scenes.support: GitHub sign-in appears only when a community feature needs identity.
scenes.proof: Your name · both previews · explicit Publish
scenes.voiceover: Only when I choose Publish does DexThemes ask for GitHub. I review the name and both previews, approve the public action, and my verified GitHub identity is credited as the creator.

scenes.overlay: Your themes. Your ranks. Your wins.
scenes.support: Signed-in creators can see their community progress in the same flow.
scenes.proof: Repeat wins stay in stats · rewards unlock once
scenes.voiceover: Signed-in creators see achievements, ranks, and every daily or weekly win. A theme can win repeatedly and its totals keep growing, while each achievement reward unlocks only once.

scenes.support: OpenAI is nothing without its people.
scenes.proof: Unlocks Human Spark · only eligibility is stored
scenes.voiceover: Eligible OpenAI builders unlock Human Spark and the achievement, OpenAI is nothing without its people. GitHub verifies an openai.com address; DexThemes stores only eligibility, never the address.

scenes.support: Open-source feedback stays reviewable and user-controlled.
scenes.proof: Nothing opens on GitHub until you choose it
scenes.voiceover: Because DexThemes is open source, I can review feedback and explicitly open a GitHub issue from the same conversation.

scenes.overlay: Personal for you. Discoverable by everyone.
scenes.support: One conversation from idea to community.
scenes.proof: GPT-5.6 interprets · DexThemes validates · Codex previews and acts
scenes.voiceover: Every theme starts personal and can become something the Codex community discovers. GPT-5.6 interprets the idea, DexThemes validates it, and the visual app keeps creation, preview, and sharing in one conversation.
